knownBug: Existing FAQ #3 'How long does a wood gutter last?' is the fence FAQ pasted in — wood gutters are not a real residential product. Fix as part of Phase 3 template rewrite.

currentFAQs.q: How much does a gutter cost in {{CITY}}?
currentFAQs.a: Most homeowners in {{CITY}} pay between {{AVG_LOW}} and {{AVG_HIGH}} for gutter installation, depending on material and yard size.

currentFAQs.q: What is the cheapest gutter material?
currentFAQs.a: Aluminum gutters are the most affordable option at $6-$12 per linear foot installed. Vinyl is comparable at $5-$11 per linear foot but less durable in extreme climates.

currentFAQs.q: How long does a wood gutter last?
currentFAQs.a: A well-maintained wood gutter typically lasts 15-20 years. Regular staining and sealing every 2-3 years can significantly extend its lifespan. Cedar and redwood last longer than pine.
